rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL JENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. prior to the hearing, a supplement must be filed that states: 1. whether Marven Rowlette died testate or intestate, and if testate, a copy of his will must be attached; 2. identifying the intestate heirs of Vera Rowlette; and 3. attaching a 13100 affidavit from Norman House (see LRSF 14.67.I). in addition, counsel must bring to the hearing a revised order that lists the actual distributions in #7 of order section, not just in the findings section, with a new omnibus clause created as #8.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHEILA SAUKAY WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the "First Amendment to the Petition for Final Distribution," etc., was in fact a fully amended petition, not just an amendment to the petition, the Notice of Hearing did not list the documents to be heard on the FACE of the notice but on an attachment, the attorney costs did not reflect the dates such costs were incurred and were not broken down to show the filing fee separately from the service fee to file the pleadings, the Corrected I&A filed on 11/25/19 still shows that no Probate Referee was required, when a Probate Referee was required for this estate, was appointed, and appraised certain assets, and the proposed fractional interests in the real property are not entirely accurate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NETTIE M. KRAWCZYK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 12/17/19. appearance required on that date. no later than noon on 12/11/19, petitioner must file a list of all expenses for which he was reimbursed, as the accounting has no itemized list, and the total of expenses in the objections is less than the total reimbursement in the petition. receipts for the airfare, hotel stay and meals must be attached. in a verified supplement, please explain why it would be appropriate for the estate to pay for a UHaul from SF to Chicago (with related expenses), when the I & A stated the personal property had no value. at best, those expenses appear to be related to a personal benefit. counsel may be asked to explain her claim that preparing a court accounting would be expensive and take several months. NOTE-->no continuance request will be granted without an appearance. if petitioner cannot provide the receipts, the court will proceed accordingly.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRENCE HAROLD HULL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. appearance required. No new examiner notes sent. Issue: A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. (LRSF 14.37).
